definition: A European Union law designed to ensure that products consumed in the EU do not contribute to deforestation or forest degradation worldwide.
A process by which human activities transform the natural landscape, altering its ecological characteristics and functions.
The identification or estimation of the real-world geographic location of an object, such as a mobile phone or an internet-connected computer terminal.
A cryptographic hash function that generates a unique, fixed-size 256-bit (32-byte) hash value, commonly used for data integrity and security.

term: Department of Agriculture, Forestry and Fisheries (DAFF)
definition: A former department of the South African government responsible for agriculture, forestry, and fisheries. Its functions are now primarily handled by the Department of Agriculture, Land Reform and Rural Development.
